assertions.expectation: Categorizes notifications by type: auto-upgrade scheduled, new version available, end-of-life/deprecation, security patch
assertions.reason: Response clearly categorizes all four notification types: auto-upgrade scheduled (HIGH PRIORITY), new version available (MEDIUM/INFORMATIONAL), end-of-life warnings (CRITICAL), and security patches (CRITICAL)

assertions.expectation: Assigns urgency levels to each category (security patches = highest, new version available = lowest)
assertions.reason: Response uses color-coded urgency levels with security patches and EOL warnings as CRITICAL (highest), auto-upgrades as HIGH PRIORITY, version updates as MEDIUM, and new version announcements as INFORMATIONAL (lowest)

assertions.expectation: End-of-life warnings require action: plan upgrade before the support window closes
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states EOL warnings require 'Schedule upgrade immediately' and notes 'Usually 30-60 days before EOL' with impact that cluster will stop receiving security patches

assertions.expectation: Auto-upgrade scheduled notifications should trigger pre-upgrade checklist execution
assertions.reason: Response provides detailed 'Pre-Upgrade Checklist' with specific items to verify and includes actions like 'Verify maintenance windows are appropriate' and 'Test applications against target version'

assertions.expectation: Security patch notifications may require expedited upgrades outside normal maintenance windows
assertions.reason: Response categorizes security patches as CRITICAL with 'Timeline: As soon as possible' and 'Action: Apply immediately or verify auto-upgrade is enabled', indicating urgency beyond normal schedules

assertions.expectation: New version available notifications are informational — use for planning, not immediate action
assertions.reason: Response categorizes 'New Version Announcements' as INFORMATIONAL with 'No Immediate Action' and suggests 'Review release notes, plan testing' for planning purposes

assertions.expectation: Recommends setting up Pub/Sub or Cloud Monitoring alerts for GKE upgrade events
assertions.reason: Response mentions monitoring integration with example alerts but does not specifically recommend Pub/Sub or Cloud Monitoring alerts for GKE upgrade events, only shows a generic monitoring alert example

assertions.expectation: Mentions GKE release notes as the source for understanding what changed in each version
assertions.reason: Response specifically mentions 'Review release notes and breaking changes' in the Pre-Upgrade Checklist and 'Review release notes, plan testing' for new version announcements
